note: Seed of the public validation suite. Every case is synthetic with a fully published derivation, so inputs and expected values can be redistributed freely. NIST/SEMATECH e-Handbook datasets (public domain) are the next wave; AIAG MSA examples will be verified privately and never shipped (copyright).

cases.source: Hand derivation; formulas per Montgomery 8th ed. ch. 6.4
cases.derivation: x=[10,12,11,13,12]: mean=11.6; MRs=[2,1,2,1], MRbar=1.5; sigma=MRbar/d2(2) with d2(2)=2/sqrt(pi), so sigma=1.5*sqrt(pi)/2; I-limits=11.6 +/- 3*sigma; MR UCL=D4(2)*MRbar with D4(2)=1+3*d3(2)/d2(2), d3(2)=sqrt(2-4/pi).

cases.source: Hand derivation; formulas per Montgomery 8th ed. ch. 6.2
cases.derivation: Subgroups [[10,12],[11,13],[9,11],[12,14],[10,12]] (n=2, k=5): means=[11,12,10,13,11], xbarbar=11.4; ranges all 2, Rbar=2; sigma=Rbar/d2(2)=sqrt(pi); xbar limits=11.4 +/- 3*sigma/sqrt(2); R UCL=D4(2)*Rbar.

cases.source: Hand derivation; formulas per Montgomery 8th ed. ch. 6.3
cases.derivation: Same subgroups: s of each pair {a,a+2} = sqrt(2), sbar=sqrt(2); sigma=sbar/c4(2) with c4(2)=sqrt(2/pi), so sigma=sqrt(pi); xbar limits as in xbar_r; S UCL=B4(2)*sbar with B4(2)=1+3*sqrt(1-c4^2)/c4.
